summary: Moved most of the ftCo_CargoThrow block out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into a new C translation unit and header, adding the CargoWait2 and CargoThrow directional callbacks for forward/back/high/low/air. The PR report shows the new file at 99.24% overall: every listed function matched 100% except ftCo_8009BF3C, which remained partially matched at 94.58% and was guarded with an ASM/MUST_MATCH fallback. Supporting changes added the new object to obj_files.mk, included ftCo_CargoThrow.h from neighboring cargo action files, tightened prototypes in ft_08A4.h, added a FighterEvent typedef, and lightly refactored ftCo_ItemThrow inline helpers to align with the same motion-transition callback pattern.

key_files.role: new C implementation for CargoWait2 and CargoThrow callbacks; 328 added lines; report says 99.24% file match
new public header with address-commented prototypes for CargoWait2 and CargoThrow Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks
removed 526 lines of assembly corresponding to the ported CargoThrow block
refactored inline motion-state helper shape and added MUST_MATCH unused stack padding around ftCo_80096250
updated prototypes for ftCo_800DD724 and ftCo_800DE3FC and removed CargoThrow-local static declarations
added typedef void (*FighterEvent)(Fighter* fp) for callback helper reuse

what_changed: Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CargoThrow.c and removed the corresponding CargoThrow assembly from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.

Implemented CargoWait2 callbacks: ftCo_CargoWait2_Anim calls ftCo_8008F744 then chooses ftCo_8009BC58 or ftCo_8009B518 based on x221C_b6 and ground_or_air; ftCo_CargoWait2_Phys delegates to ftCo_Damage_Phys; ftCo_CargoWait2_Coll handles ground/air transitions via ft_80082708, ft_80081D0C, ftCommon_8007D5D4, and ftCommon_8007D7FC.

Implemented directional CargoThrow callback wrappers for F/B/Hi/Lw/Air. Many IASA/Phys/Anim functions delegate directly to existing normal throw helpers such as ftCo_ThrowF_IASA, ftCo_ThrowB_Anim, ftCo_ThrowHi_Phys, and ftCo_ThrowLw_Phys.

Added helper logic for selecting cargo throw motion states from input: inlineA0 examines HSD_PAD_AB, left-stick X magnitude, facing_dir, attackhi3_stick_threshold_y, and xB0 to choose offsets +10 through +13 from fp->x2CC->x4_motion_state.
Kept ftCo_8009BF3C partially matched with an ASM bool definition under MUST_MATCH and a C/WIP version outside that mode; the report lists ftCo_8009BF3C at 94.58%.

Added ftCo_8009C02C to change motion state, refresh animation with ftAnim_8006EBA4, call ftCommon_8007E2F4(fp, 0x1FF), and notify the interacted fighter with ftCo_800DE3FC using a derived thrown motion id.

Added shared landing/air-transition helpers inlineB0 and inlineB1 that temporarily adjust facing_dir, call Fighter_ChangeMotionState with a large skip/update flag mask, restore facing_dir, and invoke ftCommon_8007E2F4.
Collision callbacks for ground cargo throws call ft_8008403C with ftCo_8009C170; the air collision callback calls ft_80082C74 with ftCo_8009C45C.
Created ftCo_CargoThrow.h and added includes for it in CargoFall, CargoJump, CargoKneebend, CargoTurn, CargoWait, and CargoWalk so neighboring cargo states can reference the new callbacks.

Refactored ftCo_ItemThrow.c inline helpers by splitting a motion-state-only helper inlineA1 from inlineA0, passing pre-offset msids instead of msid plus offset, and adding unused stack padding under MUST_MATCH.
smart_moves: Ported the block as a dedicated ftCo_CargoThrow.c file instead of leaving cargo throw code mixed inside the broad ft_08A4.s assembly bucket.
Used thin C wrappers around existing throw routines where the assembly was simply delegating, which reduced matching risk and clarified the relation between cargo throws and normal throws.
Isolated the only non-fully-matching function, ftCo_8009BF3C, behind an ASM/MUST_MATCH fallback while still documenting a readable C/WIP structure for future work.
Factored repeated transition behavior into static inline helpers, especially the facing_dir save/restore plus Fighter_ChangeMotionState flag sequence reused by collision transition callbacks.
Added proper address-commented prototypes and a dedicated header, then included that header from adjacent cargo-state files rather than relying on broad ft_08A4 declarations.
Preserved exact-match stack behavior where needed with explicit MUST_MATCH-only unused stack arrays, as seen in ftCo_8009C02C, ftCo_8009C170, and the ftCo_ItemThrow refactor.
decomp_lessons: For action-state files, match easy delegate callbacks first: Anim/IASA/Phys wrappers that call existing throw or damage helpers can often reach 100% quickly and shrink asm files substantially.

When one selector function resists matching, it is acceptable to keep only that function in inline ASM under MUST_MATCH while moving the rest of the file to C; this PR still achieved a 99.24% file report.

Cargo throw motion ids are derived from the character's base motion-state table, using offsets +10/+11/+12/+13 for directional cargo throws and +4/-4 style adjustments for ground/air transition variants.

Collision transition helpers often require preserving and temporarily overriding facing_dir before Fighter_ChangeMotionState, then restoring it afterward; this pattern is reusable in other throw/carry transitions.

Header cleanup matters during asm-to-C moves: once functions become real C symbols, local/static declarations should move out of catch-all headers into the new feature header, while only cross-file helpers remain in broader headers.
Unused stack padding under MUST_MATCH is a documented tactic for functions whose stack frame shape otherwise differs from the original.
naming_conventions: New file naming follows the ftCommon action-state pattern: ftCo_CargoThrow.c and ftCo_CargoThrow.h.
Public callbacks use ftCo_CargoThrow<Direction>_<CallbackKind>, for example ftCo_CargoThrowF_Anim, ftCo_CargoThrowB_IASA, ftCo_CargoThrowHi_Phys, ftCo_CargoThrowLw_Coll, and ftCo_CargoThrowAir_Coll.
CargoWait2 callbacks are grouped with the same suffix convention: ftCo_CargoWait2_Anim, ftCo_CargoWait2_Phys, and ftCo_CargoWait2_Coll.
Unknown/internal helpers retain address-based names such as ftCo_8009BF3C, ftCo_8009C02C, ftCo_8009C170, and ftCo_8009C45C.
Header prototypes retain address comments like /* 09BE54 */ and use ftCo_GObj* gobj parameters consistently.
The new header guard uses the address-style project convention GALE01_09BE54.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `ASM bool ftCo_8009BF3C(ftCo_GObj* gobj)` with `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` for readable C and an `#else` nofralloc assembly body for matching builds.
Inserted `#pragma peephole on` after the inline ASM section for ftCo_8009BF3C.
Used MUST_MATCH-only dummy stack arrays such as `u8 _[16] = { 0 };` and `u8 _[8] = { 0 };` to reproduce unused stack behavior.
Split repeated motion-transition code into static inline functions so compiler inlining could reproduce the original instruction structure.

Kept literal motion-state flag combinations in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls, including Ft_MF_SkipMatAnim, Ft_MF_SkipColAnim, Ft_MF_UpdateCmd, Ft_MF_SkipItemVis, Ft_MF_Unk19, Ft_MF_SkipModelPartVis, Ft_MF_SkipModelFlags, and Ft_MF_Unk27.
Used the ABS macro and p_ftCommonData stick-threshold fields to express the branch structure from the assembly's float comparisons.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the supplied PR context, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match ftCo_8009BF3C or rename its helper logic.
Search for uses of FighterEvent after PR #900 to see whether the callback typedef became a broader convention.
Look for later cleanup of inlineA0/inlineB0 helper names in ftCo_CargoThrow.c.
Compare ftCo_ItemThrow transition helper refactors with ftCo_CargoThrow inlineB0 to reuse the facing_dir save/restore pattern.
